_comment: Adi — Broadnax House workspace Slack app manifest. Apply this to the existing Adi app in the Broadnax House workspace (update manifest in place, then reinstall). Paired with deploy/slack-app.fellwork.manifest.json (shared primitives, workspace-specific extras).

display_information.name: Adi
display_information.description: Slack connector for Adi — household, family, and shared-life assistant
display_information.long_description:
Adi is a Slack-based operations and execution interface for OpenClaw that helps Shane and Meagan coordinate household life without constant context switching. In the Broadnax House workspace, Adi is tuned for the rhythms of home: family schedules, shared discipleship and church life, recipe and meal planning, chore tracking, and the logistical glue that keeps a two-career household with active kids moving. She captures requests, drafts actions, tracks progress, and reports outcomes in a form easy to scan between school pickups.

As an OpenClaw interface, Adi connects conversational requests in Slack to real tool execution, moving from plan to done in the same flow. She is proactive but controlled: safe internal operations happen automatically, external actions require explicit approval, and she holds household context persistently so no one has to repeat themselves. For shared discipleship and church life, Adi maintains shared context between Shane and Meagan, respecting the one firm boundary — gifts and surprises stay with the planner and never sync to the other person's instance.

slash_commands.description: Create a Todoist task via Adi
slash_commands.usage_hint: [task description with optional Todoist quick-add syntax]

slash_commands.description: Capture a note to your second brain
Look up a person from your second brain
Core group, couples group, Missions trip prep, shared serving context
Recipe lookup, save, or shopping list build (future API integration)
Track household chores — add, assign, mark done
Family schedule — kids' activities, appointments, events
Finance reference and household budget (future API integration)
